summary: Session 5 of 5 (final session): docs, required path-aware critique, and release preparation. Authored documentation across 5 surfaces (tier-model.md seat-profile clause, ai-led-session-workflow.md pointer, consumer-bootstrap engine-agents-tail.md pointer, repository-reference.md version-walk + release-status row, ai_router/CHANGELOG.md dated 0.28.0 section with a rollback recipe), drafted via route(task_type=documentation, gemini-pro) and hand-applied with 2 corrections after its own auto-verify (a broken relative link, a format nit) plus one adjudicated false positive (a router-config.yaml path claim, disproved by grep). Ran the required end-of-set path-aware critique via the first-party automated producer (python -m ai_router.pull_critique, default openai+google providers, natural full sandbox): both providers returned ISSUES_FOUND. openai/gpt-5.4 found a real Major -- an assistant.message event with no data key at all defaulted to an empty-dict success instead of failing closed, the same silent-false-success class Session 4 fixed for the wrong-shaped-data case, one level up; this exact question had been raised as a Suggestion in Session 4's own code review and adjudicated no-fix at the time, but a second independent reviewing surface catching the identical gap was treated as superseding that adjudication. Fixed in ai_router/cli_transport.py (the data key must be present before being treated as a payload dict) with the regression test renamed and flipped. google/gemini-2.5-pro found no new issue -- it independently re-confirmed a Session-4 code-review fabrication that Session 4 had already caught and discarded; recorded no_change_needed. Full adjudication in s5-issues.json; path-aware-critique.json saved raw and unedited. Release-ordering check: confirmed via git tag -l that neither v0.27.0 nor v0.28.0 exists yet and Set 077 is still in-progress (Session 6 of 6); per the spec's invariant, prepared the release (pyproject.toml bumped to 0.28.0, dated CHANGELOG, repository-reference entries) but pushed no tag and claimed no publish date/run-id -- both docs record the required v0.27.0-then-v0.28.0 order. End-of-session cross-provider verification (routed, gpt-5.4) ran 2 rounds: Round 1 (ISSUES_FOUND) found 4 real documentation-accuracy issues (a CHANGELOG Fixed bullet describing only the S4 behavior not the S5 refinement; a rollback recipe recommending a pip pin to 0.27.0 without noting 0.27.0 is itself still publish-pending; an engine-tail phrase reading as an overclaim against the docs' own degraded-guarantee framing; an absolute No extension changes claim that didn't account for the recompiled extension dist mirror), all fixed; Round 2 confirmed VERIFIED, all 4 resolved, no new issues. Also self-caught and fixed a build-sync defect: editing the canonical engine-file.agents-tail.md template (twice) desynced the extension's compiled dist/templates/consumer-bootstrap mirror; fixed both times via npm run compile. Final suite: 2482 passed, 5 skipped, the same single pre-existing test_real_repo_passes_all_drift_checks failure throughout this entire set (Sets 077 and 078 simultaneously in-progress, by explicit operator direction), expected to clear once this close lands. Authored change-log.md for the whole set (Sessions 1-5 summary + release status + end-of-set deliverables).
